prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde were computed using **DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (tier `standard`) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Mulliken partial charges were used to derive the indices from single-point calculations on the neutral molecule (N), cation (N−1, doublet), and anion (N+1, doublet). All SCF cycles converged. The carbon atom (index 1, 1-based) is the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.7084), and the oxygen atom (index 0, 1-based) is the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.4674). The dual descriptor correctly identifies the carbon as the dominant reactive center. Charge conservation checks passed for both f⁺ and f⁻ (sum = 1.0 in both cases). A PNG visualization of the indices has been generated.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.70839
